decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. A conservative 50% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized hot spot under the chip to meet the 85°C constraint.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
If this single change is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to combine geometry widening with further material tuning
